design_philosophy.core_loop: Defeat successive waves of enemies through melee combat before advancing to next stage; manage special attack charges and positioning
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Crowd control and resource management of limited special attacks (D. Punch charges) against escalating enemy variety
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as superhero Sonic Blastman defeating diverse evil forces (street gangs, terrorists, aliens, robots, clone) across escalating global threats from construction site to outer space
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes improvisation over pattern memorization; special grab-throw system where direction of d-pad input determines throw effect (sonic wave, whirlwind punch, or backward throw); bonus levels adapt arcade punch-pad mechanic to controller rotation, bridging arcade and console experiences

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple hits allowed per stage with damage accumulation
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; each stage is discrete challenge before advancing
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls when to use limited D. Punch charge attacks; grab system rewards positioning and timing choices

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden level select accessible via Music menu: select music track and input L, R, R, L, Start with Select held to jump to corresponding stage
replayability_hooks: Five distinct stages with different enemy types and environmental contexts
Bonus levels adapted from arcade version with alternative control scheme (d-pad rotation)
Hidden level select encourages non-linear stage exploration
Regional differences (censored vs. uncensored female enemy sprites) create content variance

world_building_techniques: Stage scenarios establish threat escalation: thug assault → baby carriage rescue → building siege → giant crab → asteroid impact
Final boss (Heavy Blast Man, evil clone) provides thematic closure to superhero narrative
Progression from Earth locations (construction site) to outer space visualizes global stakes expansion

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation against clone of protagonist (Heavy Blast Man) represents inverse of player's heroic identity; stage progression suggests bosses correspond to primary threats (implied final asteroid/space boss)
notable_bosses: Heavy Blast Man (evil clone, final boss)

danger_signals: Assault scenario establishes street-level violence baseline
Terrorist control of building indicates organized threat escalation
Giant crab terrorizing cruise ship signals environmental/creature threats
Asteroid collision threat represents existential/planetary danger
Clone antagonist suggests ideological inversion or corruption of protagonist values
environmental_storytelling: Construction site setting for street-level conflicts
Building siege location for organized threat
Outer space environment for final planetary threat
Progression geography mirrors threat escalation from local to global to cosmic scale

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Sonic Blast Man (SNES)
